zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Long Gunne III於1540年5月11日出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾沙特波利時，他的父親Sir Walter Gunn43歲，母親Mary Marchant17歲。他於1566年5月30日與Ann Fullwood在英國瓦維其可爾阿斯頓結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1602年5月25日在他的家鄉享年62歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾沙特波利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Long Gunne III于1540年5月11日出生在英国格盧斯特斯爾沙特波利时，他的父亲Sir Walter Gunn43岁，母亲Mary Marchant17岁。他于1566年5月30日与Ann Fullwood在英国瓦維其可爾阿斯頓结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1602年5月25日在他的家乡享年62岁，埋葬在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾沙特波利。

nameMeanings.name: Gunne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Mechelle, Pierre.
Variant of   Gano  , a surname of French origin.

variants.name: Gano
meanings: Altered form of French Gaineau, itself a variant of Ganeau: from the ancient Germanic personal name Gagino, from gagin ‘gain, win’. The surname Gaineau is very rare in France. Compare Ganoe and Gunnoe .

History: The Huguenot Étienne Gaineau or Gayneau from La Rochelle in Charente-Maritime, France, married Lydia Mestereau in La Rochelle in 1653, died in Staten Island, NY, in 1706. His name is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (as Étienne Gaineau or Ganeau or Geneau or Gano) and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America (as Etienne Gaineau or Gano).

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Long Gunne III was born on 11 May 1540, in Saintbury, Gloucestershire, England, his father, Sir Walter Gunn, was 43 and his mother, Mary Marchant, was 17. He married Ann Fullwood on 30 May 1566, in Aston, Warwickshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 25 May 1602, in his hometown, at the age of 62, and was buried in Saintbury, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Long Gunne III est né le 11 mai 1540, à Saintbury, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Walter Gunn, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Mary Marchant, avait 17 ans. Il a épousé Ann Fullwood le 30 mai 1566, à Aston, Warwickshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 25 mai 1602, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterré à Saintbury, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Long Gunne III am 11. Mai 1540 in Saintbury, Gloucestershire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Walter Gunn 43 und seine Mutter Mary Marchant 17. Er hat am 30. Mai 1566 in Aston, Warwickshire, England Ann Fullwood geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 25. Mai 1602 im Alter von 62 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Saintbury, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Long Gunne III è nato il il 11 maggio 1540 nel Saintbury, Gloucestershire, England, il suo padre, Sir Walter Gunn, aveva 43 anni e il suo madre, Mary Marchant, aveva 17 anni. Lui sposato Ann Fullwood il 30 maggio 1566, nel Aston, Warwickshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 25 maggio 1602, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 62 anni, e fu sepolto a Saintbury, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Long Gunne IIIが1540年5月11日にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアサイントブリーで生まれた時，父Sir Walter Gunnは43歳，母Mary Marchantは17歳であった。1566年5月30日にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアアストンでAnn Fullwoodと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1602年5月25日に故郷で62歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアサイントブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Long Gunne III(이)가 1540년 5월 11일 영국 그루스스터실르 세인트버이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Walter Gunn(은)는 43세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary Marchant(은)는 17세였다. 그는 1566년 5월 30일 영국 워윅실르 애스턴에서 Ann Fullwood(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1602년 5월 25일 그의 고향에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 세인트버이에 묻혔다.

pt.11 May 1540–25 May 1602: 11 de maio de 1540–25 de maio de 1602
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Long Gunne III nasceu em 11 de maio de 1540, em Saintbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Walter Gunn, tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Mary Marchant, tinha 17 anos. Ele casou-se com Ann Fullwood em 30 de maio de 1566, em Aston, Warwickshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 25 de maio de 1602, em sua cidade natal, com 62 anos, e foi sepultado em Saintbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Long Gunne III родился 11 мая 1540 г. в местности сеинтбури, глоусестиршир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Walter Gunn было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Mary Marchant, 17. Он женился на женщине по имени Ann Fullwood 30 мая 1566 г. в местности астон, варвиккшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 25 мая 1602 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 62 лет и был похоронен в местности сеинтбури, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.11 May 1540–25 May 1602: 11 de mayo de 1540–25 de mayo de 1602
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Long Gunne III nació el 11 de mayo de 1540, en Saintbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Walter Gunn, tenía 43 años y su mamá, Mary Marchant, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Ann Fullwood el 30 de mayo de 1566, en Aston, Warwickshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 25 de mayo de 1602, en su pueblo, a la edad de 62 años, y fue sepultado en Saintbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
